ops.title: ship pre-flight resolution + empty refusal (v0.27.0): the multi-bookmark quirk root cause
ops.body:
Root cause of the "ship fails trunk resolution after forming the commit; --bookmark retry pushes a harmless empty duplicate tip" quirk: runShip committed before shipPushJJ resolved the push target, so every resolution refusal (multi-bookmark trunk head → `cannot resolve the trunk bookmark from [...]`, non-trunk nearest, --bookmark not-found/conflicted, git detached HEAD) stranded a formed commit; the retry then ran bare `jj commit -m` on the now-empty @ (jj commits empty happily) and pushed both. Same missing guard caused the double-run empty-duplicate incidents (task 1f46157).

Fixed in 88e4dd0803e9, released v0.27.0:
- `shipPreflight{,JJ,Git}` run LOCAL-ONLY resolution refusals before the commit forms — a refusal mutates nothing (op log byte-identical, proven live). Fetch/divergence/rebase/move/push stay post-commit in `shipPushJJ`, identical to the pre-change sequence.
- `shipRefuseEmptyJJ` refuses an empty @ pre-commit (`nothing to commit — did a prior ship already land <sha>?` + validated-target push hint; no hint under --no-push). Exempt: `--amend`, hunk selections, merges (`parents > 1` — conflicted empty merges included). Conflict-inherited empty children refuse (the unlimited-duplicates hole a refuter found and we closed). Empty root describes as `000000000000 ""` (splitDescribe now splits before trimming).
- Behavior delta: parked on a tracked feature bookmark whose remote advanced, ship now refuses with `--bookmark` guidance instead of silently advancing trunk onto the feature-based stack (the old "success" was the foreign-bookmark incident class).

Review trail: sol finder + adversarial refuter (executed counterexamples) + delta refuter; three revision rounds. Docs: cc-skills fragments version-control.md + ccx.md entry 13 @ 67b7e5bc.
